raw_consumer_statement:
I prepaid for a car rental through XXXX using my Citibank credit card. When I arrived at the rental location ( XXXX ), the company had no vehicles available and could not provide the service I paid for. 
I disputed the charge as service not rendered. Citibank initially issued a provisional credit but later reversed it after accepting the merchants non-refundable policy. 

A non-refundable policy does not apply when a merchant fails to provide the contracted service. I did not cancel or no-show ; I arrived and was refused service due to lack of availability. 
I am requesting Citibank correct this error and honor consumer protections under the Fair Credit Billing Act. 

The car rental was scheduled from XX/XX/XXXX though XX/XX/XXXX. The XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  was booked as part of a XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX did not itemize or separately disclose the specific dollar amount allocated to the rental car portion of the charge. As a result, the exact rental car amount is not available. However, the service itself was not provided because the rental company had no vehicles available at pickup. The amount I am seeking is what it cost me to rent the car because XXXX was not able to provide the service I paid for. {$340.00} is the amount.
